commands.author: Takhoffman

target.title: [Bug] MiniMax Portal OAuth token cannot auto-refresh — refreshOAuth method not implemented
target.body:
## Bug Description

**MiniMax Portal OAuth tokens cannot be automatically refreshed**, causing "No credentials found" errors once the access token expires (~2 hours after initial login). Users must manually re-authenticate each time.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure MiniMax Portal OAuth via `openclaw auth` CLI
2. Wait for access token to expire (default ~2 hours)
3. Attempt any LLM request requiring MiniMax
4. Error: `No credentials found for profile \"minimax-portal:default\"`

**Expected:** Token should auto-refresh using the stored refresh token
**Actual:** Auto-refresh silently fails → system reports credential missing (even though credential exists in auth store)

## Root Cause Analysis

The MiniMax plugin (`dist/extensions/minimax/`) only implements `loginMiniMaxPortalOAuth` for the device code OAuth flow. **There is no `refreshOAuth` method** to handle token refresh when the access token expires.

The OAuth refresh flow in `oauth-BLwtD1j_.js` calls `refreshOAuthCredential()`:
1. First tries `plugin.refreshProviderOAuthCredentialWithPlugin()` → MiniMax plugin has **no refreshOAuth** → returns undefined
2. Then tries `getOAuthApiKey(provider)` via pi-ai library → **pi-ai's `BUILT_IN_OAUTH_PROVIDERS`** (`anthropic`, `github-copilot`, `gemini-cli`, `antigravity`, `openai-codex`) **does not include MiniMax** → returns null
3. Both fail silently → `doRefreshOAuthTokenWithLock()` returns null → "No credentials found"

## Technical Details

- **OpenClaw Version:** 2026.4.29 (plugin runtime: openclaw-2026.4.29-da6bdffc3d96)
- **MiniMax Plugin Files:**
  - `dist/extensions/minimax/oauth.js` — only exports `loginMiniMaxPortalOAuth`
  - `dist/extensions/minimax/oauth.runtime.js` — only re-exports `loginMiniMaxPortalOAuth`
- **Key Files:**
  - `dist/oauth-BLwtD1j_.js:485` — `refreshOAuthCredential()` tries plugin refresh then pi-ai
  - `dist/oauth-BLwtD1j_.js:312` — `doRefreshOAuthTokenWithLock()` returns null when both fail
  - `dist/model-auth-T8TwfpUv.js:279` — throws "No credentials found" when `resolveApiKeyForProfile` returns null

## Environment

- macOS (Mac mini M4)
- OpenClaw 2026.4.29 running as LaunchAgent
- MiniMax Portal OAuth profile: `minimax-portal:default`
- Token endpoint: `https://api.minimaxi.com/oauth/token` (CN) / `https://api.minimax.io/oauth/token` (Global)

## Workaround

Manual re-authentication via device code flow (requires user interaction).

## Suggested Fix

Add `refreshOAuth` implementation to the MiniMax plugin, similar to how other OAuth providers handle refresh. The refresh token endpoint exists and works — confirmed via curl test:

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.minimaxi.com/oauth/token \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "grant_type=refresh_token&client_id=78257093-7e40-4613-99e0-527b14b39113&refresh_token=<stored_refresh_token>"
```

Returns: `{"status":"success","access_token":"...","refresh_token":"...","expired_in":<timestamp>}`

## Labels

bug, oauth, minimax, provider-plugin

## Impact

High — Any MiniMax Portal user will experience service interruption every ~2 hours until manually re-authenticated.
target.author: rongtianhua
